description: A person taking time over a path can be 'sheru'. The adjective also suits a process that advances by small steps. The word gives a low rate of movement or change. Mindful attention can accompany any speed.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh' and move into true-mid front 'e'. A tap or short trill begins the second syllable before rounded high-back 'u'. Stress falls on 'she'.
usage_notes: The pattern 'sheru wepu' means go slowly. Choice or conditions may set the pace. 'reshi' (fast) gives a high rate, while 'kolu' (steady) tells whether the pace remains even.

semantic_domains.physical: a low rate of bodily or material movement
semantic_domains.temporal: an action or change that proceeds slowly
